hallucinations.description: The paper claims that models trained on BoolQ generalize best to TriviaQA and SQuAD, while models trained on SQuAD show the weakest cross-dataset transfer. However, the experimental data shows the exact opposite: models trained on BoolQ have the lowest cross-dataset AUROC (0.9953 on TriviaQA, 0.9951 on SQuAD), while models trained on SQuAD have the highest cross-dataset AUROC (0.9989 on TriviaQA, 0.9994 on BoolQ).
hallucinations.evidence: GRU and Transformer models trained on BoolQ generalize best to TriviaQA and SQuAD, while models trained on SQuAD show the weakest cross-dataset transfer—consistent with SQuAD’s weaker cascade structure identified in the main text.

overall_assessment: The paper contains a significant hallucination regarding the cross-dataset generalization results. It completely reverses the actual experimental findings, claiming that BoolQ-trained models generalize best and SQuAD-trained models generalize worst, when the data shows the opposite. Other claims and metrics in the paper are generally accurate and well-supported by the data.
